L1.area: Sandbox runtime evaluation and execution context setup

L2.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where certain function constructors are not replaced with their sandboxed equivalents. When sandboxed code accesses the constructor property of specific function types, it obtains the native host constructor instead of a safe replacement. This allows creating new functions that execute entirely outside the sandbox, enabling arbitrary code execution in the host environment.

L3.area: Sandbox execution context setup and function constructor replacement mappings
L3.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists where the sandbox replaces the synchronous function constructor with a safe sandboxed version but fails to do the same for async function constructors. When sandboxed code accesses the constructor property of an async function instance, it obtains the native host async function constructor instead of a safe replacement. This allows creating new async functions that execute outside the sandbox context, bypassing all restrictions and enabling remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The sandbox maps the synchronous Function constructor to a sandboxed replacement but omits AsyncFunction, GeneratorFunction, and AsyncGeneratorFunction from the replacement map. An attacker can access the native AsyncFunction constructor via .constructor on an async function and use it to execute arbitrary code outside the sandbox.
